quotes.quote: Across the three tasks, DeepScientist generate over 5,000 unique ideas, yet only about 1,100 are deemed worthy of experimental validation by the system's selection mechanism, and a mere 21 ultimately result in scientific progress.
quotes.rationale: An internal selection mechanism gates which of 5,000 ideas advance to experimental validation, a stage transition; substrate is not stated.

quotes.quote: Specifically, it uses the classic Upper Confidence Bound (UCB) algorithm to select the most promising record.
quotes.rationale: UCB is a deterministic selection algorithm that controls which record the search expands next.

quotes.quote: The architecture of DeepScientist actualizes the Bayesian Optimization loop through a multi-agent system equipped with an open-knowledge system and a continuously accumulating Findings Memory. This memory is composed of both frontier human knowledge (e.g., papers and codes) and the system's own historical findings, and it intelligently guides subsequent explorations.
quotes.rationale: The selection loop is a Bayesian Optimization loop guided by accumulated findings.

quotes.quote: This deeply validated record becomes a new record in the system's knowledge base, thus influencing the decision-making process in all subsequent cycles.
quotes.rationale: Validated records are admitted to the knowledge base and feed subsequent decisions.

quotes.quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.
quotes.rationale: Independent re-execution checks executability against a measured ~50% false-positive rate in the coding agent's self-reports.

quotes.quote: Three human experts supervise the process to verify outputs and filter out hallucinations.
quotes.rationale: Human experts verify outputs and filter hallucinations in-loop; routing of their verdicts is not specified.

quotes.quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.
quotes.rationale: Manual inspection of all experimental results by human supervisors.

quotes.quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.
quotes.rationale: A third-party LLM reviewer agent scores final outputs against a comparison corpus for benchmarking only.

quotes.quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.
quotes.rationale: A convened committee of volunteer researchers renders a terminal assessment of outputs.

quotes.quote: To make the AI a more capable partner, future work should focus on key enhancements: developing simulated discovery environments to accelerate learning via reinforcement, creating frameworks for integrating feedback from the scientific community, and ultimately, bridging the gap to the physical sciences through robotics.
quotes.rationale: Community-feedback integration and robotic/physical validation are stated as future work, not exercised.

diagnostics.consequence: The most consequential filter in the loop cannot be audited or reproduced, and its contribution to the 21 reported successes cannot be attributed.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Across the three tasks, DeepScientist generate over 5,000 unique ideas, yet only about 1,100 are deemed worthy of experimental validation by the system's selection mechanism, and a mere 21 ultimately result in scientific progress.

diagnostics.statement: The system's idea selection mechanism performs a high-stakes stage transition (about 1,100 of over 5,000 ideas advanced) but its substrate, criteria, and evidence source are not described in the packet.

diagnostics.consequence: Any result path not covered by re-execution or manual inspection is at high risk of being spurious, and autonomy claims depend on a human backstop.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.

diagnostics.statement: The coding agent's self-reported completion had roughly a 50% false-positive rate, requiring an independent re-execution step plus manual inspection to detect silent failures.

diagnostics.consequence: Headline quality comparisons against 28 prior-system papers rest on evaluators of unmeasured reliability who are not organizationally independent.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.

diagnostics.statement: Both terminal evaluations — the LLM reviewer agent and the author-convened program committee — are reported without any reliability study for the aggregate-quality judgments they render, and neither is routed back into the search loop.

diagnostics.consequence: Closure of the human validation channel is indeterminate, so the degree of genuine autonomy of the discovery loop cannot be established.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Three human experts supervise the process to verify outputs and filter out hallucinations.

diagnostics.statement: Human expert supervision is stated to verify outputs and filter hallucinations, but the packet does not report how supervisor verdicts are routed — whether they halt runs, edit memory, or only annotate results.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Across the three tasks, DeepScientist generate over 5,000 unique ideas, yet only about 1,100 are deemed worthy of experimental validation by the system's selection mechanism, and a mere 21 ultimately result in scientific progress.

recommendations.proposed_action: Document the idea selection mechanism's evaluator substrate, scoring criteria, and evidence inputs, and report an ablation comparing the 5,000-to-1,100 filter against an unfiltered or random-selection baseline.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Selection mechanism yields a statistically higher progress rate than random selection at matched compute
success_check.evidence_needed: Published selector specification plus a controlled ablation with per-arm counts and a significance test
success_check.observable: Rate of ideas yielding scientific progress under the described selection mechanism versus a baseline selector on the same idea pool

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A critical step in our pipeline is a secondary verification process: after Claude Code reports completion, DeepScientist independently re-executes the main script via the command line. This measure was implemented to counteract a high rate of false positives-we observed that approximately 50% of initial implementation attempts failed to complete fully due to internal timeouts within the Claude Code agent. Throughout this project, all experimental results were manually inspected by human supervisors to guarantee their authenticity.
Three human experts supervise the process to verify outputs and filter out hallucinations.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report per-stage coverage and outcomes of the secondary re-execution and human inspection steps, including how many re-executions overturned agent-reported successes and what action each supervisor verdict triggered.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Residual false-positive rate materially below the reported ~50% initial rate, with every supervisor verdict traced to a defined downstream action
success_check.evidence_needed: Logged re-execution outcomes and a supervisor intervention log tied to run identifiers
success_check.observable: Residual false-positive rate of accepted results after re-execution, and the count and downstream routing of supervisor interventions

exact_quotes.exact_quote: First, to benchmark against existing work, we employ DeepReviewer (Zhu et al., 2025a), an AI agent that simulates the human peer-review process with an external search capability, comparing DeepScientist's output against 28 publicly available papers from other AI Scientist systems. Second, for a more rigorous assessment, we convene a dedicated program committee consisting of three active LLM researchers: two volunteers who have served as ICLR reviewers and one senior volunteer who has been invited to be an ICLR Area Chair.

recommendations.proposed_action: Run a blinded paired-rating reliability study measuring agreement between the LLM reviewer agent and the human program committee on the same submissions before using their scores to claim superiority over the 28 comparison papers.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Reported agreement statistic with confidence interval, and no significant provenance effect on scores
success_check.evidence_needed: Blinded paired-rating study over the same paper set with per-rater scores released
success_check.observable: Inter-rater agreement between the LLM reviewer and the human committee, and score difference between blinded and unblinded provenance conditions

memory_structure: continuously accumulating Findings Memory containing frontier human knowledge (papers, code) and the system's own historical findings
knowledge base of deeply validated records influencing subsequent cycles
policy_type: Bayesian Optimization loop over a multi-agent system
internal idea selection mechanism gating experimental validation
